questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Which organization has been designated as the nodal agency in India for coordination with the UNESCO Memory of the World (MoW) Programme since 2014?

options.text: Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts (IGNCA)

questions.explanation: The Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts (IGNCA) has been India's nodal agency for coordination with the UNESCO MoW programme since 2014.
questions.takeaway: IGNCA, rather than the National Archives, is the specific nodal agency for the MoW programme in India.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: India's Nodal Agency (for coordination): Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts (IGNCA) since 2014.

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: The UNESCO Memory of the World (MoW) Programme specifically identifies which of the following as a priority area for facilitating the preservation of documentary heritage?

options.text: Heritage items currently held in private collections of former colonial powers.
Documentary records located in areas affected by conflict and natural disasters.
Only those manuscripts that pre-date the invention of the printing press.
Records specifically related to the administrative history of the United Nations.

questions.explanation: One of the core objectives of the MoW programme is to facilitate preservation, with a particular emphasis on documentary heritage in areas affected by conflict and/or natural disasters, where the risk of loss is highest.
questions.takeaway: The MoW programme prioritizes preservation in high-risk environments such as conflict zones and disaster-prone areas.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Objectives: Facilitate preservation (particularly in areas affected by conflict and/or natural disaster), catalyse universal access, and enhance public awareness worldwide of the significance of documentary heritage.

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes the primary rationale behind the launch of the UNESCO Memory of the World (MoW) Programme in 1992?

options.text: To establish a legal framework for the repatriation of stolen cultural artifacts.
To safeguard against collective amnesia by preserving the world's documentary heritage.
To create a commercial marketplace for the exchange of rare historical manuscripts.
To standardize the digitalization protocols for all national archives globally.

questions.explanation: The programme was initiated in 1992 with the specific philosophical and practical goal of preserving documentary heritage to safeguard humanity against 'collective amnesia'.
questions.takeaway: The MoW programme's foundational goal is to prevent the loss of human memory (collective amnesia) through heritage preservation.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: UNESCO initiated the Memory of the World Programme in 1992 to preserve documentary heritage and safeguard against collective amnesia.
